ops.title: Symlink protection for plan-current writes
ops.body: Every write that touches `~/.getaway/plans/current` (and the plan/prefs files themselves) refuses symlinks and non-regular files: `plan_current_guard()` in getaway.sh runs before `plan-new` creates `<slug>.json` and before `plan-done` mutates status, so a tampered pointer fails with exit 3 leaving no orphan plan file and no half-applied lifecycle change. Writes go mktemp-in-target-dir + atomic `mv`. CI's plan round-trip covers the symlink case end to end (refusal, victim intact, no orphan, clean retry). When adding any new command that writes under `~/.getaway`, reuse these guards — never a bare redirect to a path an attacker could pre-plant.
